# Architecture

## Auto-evolve activation defers observer threading and event filtering to Stage B

[Trigger]: When designing the multi-stage activation plan for the auto-evolve subsystem. [Detail]: Stage A focuses on verifying existing rule promotion end-to-end without observer dependencies. Stage B builds the observer subprocess and implements per-project event filtering using kg_sync_state checkpoints. This separation allows Stage A to validate promotion logic independently. Observer runs as a separate hourly launchd service. [Consequence]: Building the observer in Stage A introduces circular dependencies and makes testing promotion impossible without a working observer.

_Source: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-12-auto-evolve-activation.md`_

_Tags: `backend`, `architecture`_

## Observer state tracked via kg_sync_state KV table with per-project keys

[Trigger]: When designing the observer subprocess to track progress per project incrementally. [Detail]: Observer maintains state in kg_sync_state using keys like `observer_last_run_at_${project_id}` and `observer_last_event_id_${project_id}`. On each run, the observer reads the last-run timestamp (defaulting to 24h ago if absent), processes new events since that point, detects patterns, and persists the high-water mark back. This enables resumable processing. [Consequence]: Manual kg_sync_state edits cause events to be re-analyzed or skipped silently with no alerting.

_Source: `src/evolve/export-events.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `architecture`_

## Auto-evolve activation uses 3-stage sequential process

[Trigger]: When activating the auto-evolve subsystem in a mature Open Pulse installation with existing rules. [Detail]: Activation occurs in three stages: Stage A verifies existing rule promotion end-to-end via src/evolve/promote.js; Stage B fixes generateComponent for agent case and validates skill/agent promotion; Stage C builds the missing observer as a standalone hourly launchd service using Haiku for pattern detection. Each stage gates the next. [Consequence]: Attempting to build the observer before Stage B completes results in agents being incorrectly auto-promoted. Skipping verification causes silent failures and corrupted .claude/ component files.

_Source: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-12-auto-evolve-activation.md`_

_Tags: `backend`, `architecture`_

## Plan generation uses dual-table instrumentation: daily_reviews + pipeline_runs

[Trigger]: When implementing async features that invoke Claude CLI and need both operational tracking and result persistence. [Detail]: Plan generation creates two independent rows: insertPipelineRun() logs the Claude CLI invocation (model, status, tokens, duration) in pipeline_runs. savePlan() stores the actual plan output and context in daily_reviews as plan_md, handoff_prompt, plan_status. This separation allows analyzing LLM costs (pipeline_runs) independently from plan content (daily_reviews). [Consequence]: Queries for plan results read daily_reviews; queries for operational costs read pipeline_runs. Deleting pipeline_runs data doesn't affect plan content, and vice versa.

_Source: `src/routes/daily-reviews.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `database`_

## savePlan stores generated plan with handoff context linked to daily review

[Trigger]: When plan generation completes asynchronously and needs to persist results. [Detail]: savePlan(db, id, planMd, handoffPrompt, runId) updates the daily_reviews row with plan_md (generated markdown), handoff_prompt (the context sent to Claude), plan_status='done', and plan_run_id (FK to pipeline_runs for cost tracking). The handoff_prompt is stored for auditability and to show users what context was used for the plan. [Consequence]: Missing handoff_prompt storage loses auditability; missing plan_run_id breaks cost tracking integration.

_Source: `src/review/queries.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `database`_

## Plan generation spawns async background process instead of blocking HTTP response

[Trigger]: When plan generation is triggered from daily review suggestion detail view. [Detail]: The route handler returns 202 Accepted with a task ID immediately after spawning a detached child process. The child process runs src/review/plan.js generatePlan() which invokes Claude CLI via callClaude with stdin piping for large prompts, then updates the daily_reviews table with results. The Fastify event loop is never blocked — the response returns before Claude CLI completes. [Consequence]: Frontend must poll GET /api/daily-reviews/:id/plan-status to check completion; without polling, users won't know when the plan is ready.

_Source: `src/routes/daily-reviews.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `api`_

## Plan generation module structure: plan.js exports async generatePlan for detached spawning

[Trigger]: When plan generation is triggered from daily review suggestion detail view via POST /api/daily-reviews/:id/plan/generate. [Detail]: src/review/plan.js exports generatePlan(db, suggestionId, options) as an async function. The route handler calls spawn('node', ['src/review/plan.js', suggestionId], {detached: true, stdio: 'ignore'}). The spawned process imports and invokes generatePlan directly as a standalone script with process.argv. [Consequence]: Tightly coupling the exported async function to the spawned script interface prevents using generatePlan from other contexts (tests, other features).

_Source: `src/review/plan.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `architecture`_

## Knowledge extraction pipelines share callClaude wrapper from extract.js

[Trigger]: When adding new parameters or constraints to knowledge extraction (scan, extract, or future pipelines). [Detail]: Both scan.js and extract.js import and use the callClaude() function exported from extract.js. This centralization enables consistent timeout handling, token tracking (via parseTokenUsage), and now effort control across all knowledge extraction modes. [Consequence]: New parameters (like effort, max-tokens, or model selection) only need to be added to callClaude() once to take effect across scan, extract, and any future knowledge pipelines.

_Source: `src/knowledge/extract.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `architecture`_

## Cold-start scan and post-ingest extract use different claude invocation abstractions

[Trigger]: When modifying the Claude CLI invocation for knowledge operations. [Detail]: src/knowledge/scan.js (cold-start) and src/knowledge/extract.js (post-ingest) both invoke Claude CLI, but via different abstractions. scan.js calls callClaude(prompt, model, { effort: 'max' }) exported from extract.js. Both use the same callClaude wrapper for consistency. The wrapper handles --output-format json parsing and stderr token extraction. [Consequence]: Changes to Claude CLI invocation must be coordinated across both files — a bug in callClaude affects both cold-start scan and post-ingest extraction.

_Source: `src/knowledge/extract.js`_

_Tags: `backend`_

## Plan generation buildPlanPrompt enforces maxKb limit by breaking BEFORE exceeding, not AFTER

[Trigger]: When buildPlanPrompt() assembles candidate file paths and content for the plan context. [Detail]: The loop iterates through candidatePaths, accumulating bytes. When the next file would exceed maxBytes, the loop breaks immediately—the last file is NOT added. This prevents the LAST file from exceeding the limit, unlike naive implementations that add first then check. [Consequence]: If the break condition is removed or inverted, the final file will exceed maxKb and truncate plan context silently.

_Source: `src/review/plan.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `performance`_

## Plan generation feature uses dual-table instrumentation: daily_reviews + pipeline_runs

[Trigger]: When implementing async features that invoke Claude CLI and need both operational tracking and result persistence. [Detail]: Plan generation records two rows: (1) pipeline_runs row inserted at start, updated at completion, tracks execution time and tokens; (2) daily_reviews row updated with plan_md and plan_status when complete. These tables serve different purposes: pipeline_runs tracks operational cost/timing, daily_reviews stores the result. [Consequence]: Using only one table loses either the result (if only pipeline_runs) or execution metadata (if only daily_reviews).

_Source: `src/review/plan.js`_

_Tags: `database`, `backend`_

## Auto-evolve activation requires three sequential stages with observer architecture

[Trigger]: When activating the auto-evolve subsystem after observer.js deletion. [Detail]: Auto-evolve activation proceeds in three stages: Stage A verifies existing rule promotion works end-to-end, Stage B fixes generateComponent for agent/skill types and tests promotion, Stage C implements the missing observer as a standalone hourly launchd service. The observer uses Haiku to detect patterns in events, generates new instinct YAML files, and promotes to rules/skills when confidence >= 0.85. Agents are blacklisted from auto-promotion and surfaced via a separate manual promote endpoint. [Consequence]: Without this staged approach, promoting agents or skills will fail silently, and the observer will never run without launchd configuration.

_Source: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-12-auto-evolve-activation-design.md`_

_Tags: `architecture`, `backend`, `deployment`_
